scenario: Nimbus Insurance integrates a Camunda Spring Boot Starter worker with a self-managed Camunda 8.8 cluster that authenticates via OpenID Connect. The cluster expects 5200 jobs per day and the team configures camunda.client.auth.client-id and camunda.client.auth.client-secret without setting camunda.client.auth.method explicitly. A reviewer asks which authentication method will actually be applied in this configuration.
question: Which authentication method does the starter activate when only client-id and client-secret are configured?

options.text: The starter activates the none preset because camunda.client.auth.method was not set explicitly, so the worker connects without sending any credentials to the self-managed cluster
The starter activates the basic preset because client-id and client-secret are accepted as username and password aliases when camunda.client.auth.method is omitted from configuration
The starter refuses to start because camunda.client.auth.method is required whenever any auth property is set, so the worker fails fast at context initialization with an error
The starter activates the oidc preset because the oidc method is implied when either camunda.client.auth.client-id or camunda.client.auth.client-secret is set in the application properties

a.text: Incorrect. The none preset is only the default when no other property implies an authentication method; setting client-id or client-secret implies oidc.

b.text: Incorrect. Basic auth is implied by camunda.client.auth.username or camunda.client.auth.password, not by client-id or client-secret.

c.text: Incorrect. The starter detects the auth method automatically when camunda.client.auth.method is omitted; it does not require the method to be set explicitly.

d.text: Correct. The docs state that the oidc method will be implied if you set either camunda.client.auth.client-id or camunda.client.auth.client-secret, even without setting method explicitly.

explanation: When camunda.client.auth.method is omitted, the starter detects the method from other properties. Setting camunda.client.auth.client-id or camunda.client.auth.client-secret implies the oidc method, while setting username or password implies basic, and otherwise the none preset is used.
